summary: The patient likely suffers from "anemia of chronic disease" secondary to her long-standing RA condition which leads to low hemoglobin despite having higher-than-normal ferritin indicating underlying systemic inflammation rather than absolute deficiency or other rare etiologies such as thalassemia traits. This scenario fits well within known presentations where autoimmunity drives persistent mild-to-moderate hematological changes without classic signs pointing directly back only one narrow diagnostic
